# awesome-cli-apps submission packet (ble)

Prepared 2026-07-19. **Do not submit until the app qualifies** — see gates below.
Our first attempt (PR #1244) was auto-closed for not using the template.

## Eligibility gates (both required by their contributing.md)

- [ ] **Age > 3 months** — repo created 2026-07-19, so eligible **on/after 2026-10-19**.
- [ ] **Stars > 20** — currently 0. Needs 21+.

Also: their guidelines say **AI-generated PRs are not welcome** and they want a
*human's* genuine reasoning. So **you** open the PR from your account and write the
"Why I think it's awesome" section in your own words. (That's the one part left blank
below — it has to be yours, not drafted.)

## Where it goes

File `readme.md`, section **Utilities → macOS**, as the **last** entry (after `anvil`).

## Exact entry line (format-compliant: capital start, ends with period, no "CLI"/"terminal", no trailing space)

```
- [ble](https://github.com/kylebrowning/BLESwiftCLI) - Scan, connect, pair, read, write, and inspect Bluetooth Low Energy peripherals.
```

## PR

- **Title (exactly):** `Add ble`
- **One app per PR** ✔ (this is the only one)
- **Use their template** and fill it in:

```
#### New App Submission

- [x] I've read the [contribution guidelines](https://github.com/agarrharr/awesome-cli-apps/blob/master/contributing.md).

**Repo or homepage link:**
https://github.com/kylebrowning/BLESwiftCLI

**Description:**
Scan, connect, pair, read, write, and inspect Bluetooth Low Energy peripherals.

**Why I think it's awesome:**
<YOUR WORDS HERE — a genuine sentence or two on why you find it useful. e.g. what
it let you do that other tools couldn't, real hardware you debugged with it, etc.>
```

## Steps when the time comes

1. Sync your fork's master with upstream (or re-fork fresh), branch off it.
2. Add the entry line as the last item under Utilities → macOS.
3. Open the PR titled `Add ble`, using the template above with your own "why".
